LDTEXT: On site to check pump 1 RTD fault. Found RTD 6 for motor windings failed high. Turned off fault for RTD 6 on multilin relay. Fault has been reset, test ran pump 1 with the help of operations, pump ran without fault. Pump 1 is to be left off but is available to run if needed in an emergency. After checking all the wiring connections, RTD 6 reading returned to normal. Enabled RTD 6 faults on multilin. Asked operations to put pump1 back in auto duty for testing. If RTD 6 fault happens again we will come back and disable fault and leave pump 1 in off and for emergency use only.
DESCRIPTION: Newmarket Sewage Pumping Station & Equalization Tank

LDTEXT:
on site in response to surge tank hihi/lolo alarms, all pumps unavailable due to inhibit. 

Upon arrival, found wet well emergency ventilation running along with the normal ventilation which caused cold outside air to blow into the screen room. Also found water pipe burst next to the bay door which sprayed over the surge tank level sensors and froze them. Immediately shut off emergency ventilation fan. Put both surge tanks in maintenance bypass mode to clear the inhibits on the pumps. Placed the pump back in auto and monitoring the surge tank level to see if level sensors would come back to life. Called ROC to call INST on-call as these sensors need to be checked.

Rick (INST) on-site to take a look at the sensor down in the wet well. Flushed out debris/ice from the sensor flushing port and sensors now reading normal values. Put both surge tanks back in service. Put all pumps back in service/auto. OFF/LOTO breaker switch for wet well emergency ventilation to prevent it from coming on again.
DESCRIPTION: Newmarket Sewage Pumping Station & Equalization Tank

On site with David D and Dan H to investigate power loss

After arriving on site and having voltage confirmed from Hydro  over night found there was good utility voltage

Found phase module in ats in fault mode calling for generator to run
Phase sensing module in ats was set between 450-600VAC 

Incoming voltage was between 608-612VAC

Found phase module interrupting contact wired backwards, reversed contacts and increased high voltage sensing module to 640VAC

Now when incoming utility is between 450-640VAC there should be no power interruption to switch to generator power

Why this problem just became more prevalent is not understood. Will continue to monitor station for power loss

We will be coming back to do a voltage coordination between soft starts and low voltage setpoints on ats module when scheduled.

On site to do more operational testing with the generator while pumps are running. Tested two pumps running in duty one and killed main power to the station and observed the automatic response. Found that once the generator was up and running that multiple pumps were starting simultaneously. This caused the generator to bog down and fault out. Found that as long as only one pump starts at a time with a few minutes in between starting subsequent pumps, the generator operates correctly. Will follow up with the PCS department to see if we can stagger the start commands for each pump.
Also, input correct times into the clock setting of all multilin protection relays.
